source: Gaza Ministry of Health / UN OCHA
note: Gaza MoH figures widely cited by UN as broadly reliable for total count. Israel disputes civilian/combatant ratio. IDF says ~17,000 militants killed. Over 10,000 remain missing under rubble per MoH.

source: Gaza MoH / UNICEF / Save the Children
note: UNICEF called Gaza the most dangerous place in the world for children. Highest child death toll in any conflict in the UN's recorded history. More children killed in first months than in all global conflicts combined in 2022.
WHO documented over 700 attacks on health facilities in Gaza since Oct 7. Over 100 hospitals and clinics put out of service. Deadliest conflict for healthcare workers in WHO's history.

Highest journalist death toll in any conflict in CPJ's 40-year history. Includes Palestinian, Israeli, and foreign nationals. Many killed with press vest on; IDF investigations rarely resulted in accountability.
Single largest loss of UN staff in any conflict. UNRWA bore most of the casualties. UN Secretary-General called for accountability. Israel said some UNRWA staff were Hamas members.
Deadliest attack on Jews since the Holocaust. ~364 killed at Nova music festival; ~400 soldiers and police killed; ~400+ civilians in kibbutzim and towns. 251 hostages taken to Gaza.
Includes combat deaths in Gaza and related operations. IDF also lost dozens in West Bank operations and Hezbollah exchanges. Many deaths from explosive devices and urban ambushes.

source: Israeli PM Office / Hostages Families Forum
note: ~251 taken Oct 7; ~105 released in Nov 2023 deal; 33 released in Phase 1 ceasefire Jan-Mar 2025; ~30 died in captivity (confirmed); ~59 total remaining as of Mar 2026 (est. 24 alive).

source: B'Tselem / UN OCHA / Palestinian Ministry of Health
note: Deadliest period in West Bank since Second Intifada. IDF operations in Jenin, Tulkarm, Nablus. Also Israeli settler attacks on Palestinian villages. IDF says operations targeted militants; human rights groups document civilian deaths.
~3,354 Palestinians killed (including combatants), ~874 Israelis killed (mostly civilians in suicide bombings). Deadliest sustained conflict between the two peoples in modern times until 2023.

category: Operation Protective Edge — Gaza War III (2014)
note: UN found 1,462 Palestinian civilians killed including 551 children. IDF estimated higher combatant ratio. Most destructive Gaza war prior to 2023. $8.4B damage to Gaza infrastructure.

category: Operation Cast Lead — Gaza War I (2008–09)
note: UN Goldstone Report documented civilian casualties and alleged war crimes by both sides. IDF subsequently disputed numbers. 10 of 13 Israelis killed were soldiers; 4 by friendly fire.

source: Benny Morris historical research / Israeli Government
note: ~6,000 Israelis killed (~4,000 soldiers, ~2,000 civilians); ~5,000-8,000 Arab soldiers and Palestinian fighters killed. ~700,000–750,000 Palestinians displaced (the Nakba). Arab states' casualty figures vary widely.

~1,162 Palestinians killed by Israeli forces; ~160 Israelis killed by Palestinians. Tens of thousands injured, arrested, or subjected to 'break the bones' policy. Gaza refugee camps bore highest casualties.
Over 13 months of protests; Israeli snipers fired live ammunition at protesters. UN Commission found Israel violated international human rights law. No Israeli casualties from protesters' actions.
UN confirmed 67 children among Palestinian dead. 13 Israelis killed; 10 civilians (including 2 Thai workers). Both sides fired into civilian areas. 11-day war; Egyptian-mediated ceasefire.
